policy.pii: zero PII; aggregate statistics only; no passenger names, mobile numbers, or actual PNRs reused

anchoring_definition.REAL_ANCHORED: scenario parameter (timing, station code, train number range, language register, refund slab, scam pattern) is sampled from or constrained by a public distribution captured below
anchoring_definition.SYNTHETIC_PLAUSIBLE: scenario parameters constructed for ops-coverage and adversarial edge cases; consistent with public sources but not directly drawn from them

sources.name: Indian Railway Stations dataset
sources.publisher: Kaggle community (mirrors of RailwayAPI / NTES public station list)
sources.license: CC0 / Public Domain (Kaggle community release; underlying data is published by Indian Railways NTES which is a Govt of India public information system)
facts: 8000+ active station codes across 17 zones
Top 5 zonal hubs by passenger throughput: NDLS, HWH, CSTM, MAS, SBC
Station codes are 2-5 uppercase ASCII letters (e.g., SBC=Bangalore City Jn, MAS=Chennai Central, BCT=Mumbai Central)
Bangalore-Chennai and Bangalore-Mumbai are among the top 20 OD pairs by daily train count
Karnataka Express (12627/12628) and Lalbagh Express (12607/12608) are real, high-frequency trains on these corridors
distributions_used: station-code character pattern (3-letter uppercase) used to validate SBC/MAS/BCT in scenarios
OD-pair plausibility filter (BLR<->MAS, BLR<->BCT both top-20)

sources.name: IRCTC Trains Time-Table dataset
sources.publisher: Kaggle (multiple author mirrors of public NTES timetable)
sources.license: CC0 / public-domain mirror of Govt of India NTES timetable
facts: Train numbers are 5 digits; long-distance Mail/Express in 1xxxx range, premium (Rajdhani/Shatabdi/Vande Bharat) in 2xxxx, passenger 5xxxx
Karnataka Express 12627 SBC->NDLS departs SBC ~19:20 IST (real schedule)
Lalbagh Express 12607 SBC->MAS departs SBC ~06:30 IST
Class hierarchy: 1A > 2A > 3A > SL > 2S > GN; ~70% of long-distance bookings are SL/3A (mass-market tiers)
Charting cutoff: typically 4 hours before scheduled departure for originating station
distributions_used: class-mix prior P(SL)=0.45, P(3A)=0.28, P(2A)=0.15, P(1A)=0.04, P(2S/GN)=0.08 -> drives scenario class selection
departure-time bucket (early-morning 04-08 IST 22%, morning 08-12 22%, afternoon 12-16 14%, evening 16-20 24%, night 20-04 18%)

sources.name: IRCTC Tatkal opening windows and surge patterns
sources.publisher: IRCTC official help pages, RailYatri / RailRadar public dashboards, @IRCTCofficial Twitter advisories
sources.license: Public information (Govt of India IRCTC help pages); aggregate dashboards are RailYatri/RailRadar editorial
facts: AC Tatkal (1A/2A/3A/CC/EC) opens at 10:00 IST, one day before journey date (D-1)
Sleeper Tatkal (SL/2S) opens at 11:00 IST, one day before journey date (D-1)
Quota typically 10-30% of coach seats, varies by train
Peak surge: first 90 seconds after window open consume 60-80% of Tatkal quota on popular trains
Bookings outside the official window must be rejected by the system (adversarial test surface)
distributions_used: Tatkal-window time constants (10:00 / 11:00 IST) used as oracle for adversarial T4 'outside window' scenarios
Hour-of-day surge weight peaking at 10:00-10:02 and 11:00-11:02 IST

sources.name: CERT-In advisories on IRCTC fake-refund and impersonation scams
sources.publisher: Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In), MeitY, Govt of India
sources.url: https://www.cert-in.org.in/ (public advisories archive); PIB releases on IRCTC phishing alerts
sources.license: Govt of India open public advisory
facts: CERT-In and IRCTC have issued repeated advisories warning of fake-refund SMS/WhatsApp messages with shortened URLs
Common modus operandi: fake 'cancellation refund pending' message with URL like railway-rebooking[.]xyz / irctc-refund[.]top requesting card/UPI details
IRCTC officially never asks for card OTP or UPI PIN over phone/SMS for refunds
Refunds for cancellations are auto-credited to original payment instrument; no manual link click required
Reported phishing-domain TLDs over-represented: .xyz, .top, .online, .info
distributions_used: phishing-URL TLD distribution informs adversarial 'fake refund link' patterns
modus-operandi prior used to ensure adversarial scenarios reflect real fraud surface (not synthetic)

sources.name: SemEval-2020 Task 9 SentiMix Hindi-English code-mixed corpus
sources.publisher: SemEval / academic release; Kaggle and GitHub mirrors
sources.license: Research / non-commercial academic (used here for distributional statistics only, no text reproduction)
facts: Average code-mixing index (CMI) ~0.36 for Hindi-English transactional text
Token-level distribution in Hinglish transactional register: ~58% Hindi (Devanagari or romanized) / ~42% English
Common Hindi function tokens: kal, mujhe, kar do, chahiye, abhi, kyun, kab, mera/meri
Switch points cluster at verb phrases ('book kar do', 'cancel karo', 'check kar do')
Latin-script (romanized Hindi) dominates over Devanagari in chat/voice-assistant register (>90%)
distributions_used: 58/42 Hindi/English token ratio anchors all T3 hi-en scenarios
switch-point pattern at verb (English noun/verb + Hindi auxiliary 'kar do/karo') used in 6/6 T3 user_messages

sources.name: PhishTank verified phishing URL feed (IRCTC-impersonation slice)
sources.license: Open via API; aggregate counts only used here
facts: IRCTC-impersonation domains follow patterns: irctc-<verb>[.]<tld>, railway-<verb>[.]<tld>, indianrail-<verb>[.]<tld>
Verbs in URL: refund, rebooking, cancel, helpdesk, support
Top abused TLDs: .xyz, .top, .online, .info, .live (long-tail >30 TLDs)
Median lifespan of an IRCTC-phish domain on PhishTank: <72 hours before takedown
Volume spikes correlate with festival travel windows (Diwali, Chhath, summer vacation)
distributions_used: URL pattern template (railway-rebooking[.]xyz family) anchors any adversarial scam-link scenario

scenario_anchoring.rationale: SBC<->MAS is a real top-20 OD pair; Lalbagh Express 12607 is a real train with verifiable schedule
Karnataka Express 12627 is a real train; SL class matches modal P(SL)=0.45
PNR is fictional 10-digit; format matches IRCTC convention but not drawn from any real booking
Cancellation refund slab (>48h = full refund minus clerkage) is the published IRCTC rule
2A->SL fallback reflects real class-mix prior (P(2A)=0.15 < P(SL)=0.45 capacity)
Partial-refund window (12h to 4h before departure = 50% deduction) is published IRCTC slab
Upgrade flow constructed for ops coverage; class transition consistent with real product
Compound book+verify flow constructed for multi-op coverage
Senior-citizen lower-berth allotment is a real IRCTC priority rule (age >= 60)
Tatkal SL window (11:00 IST D-1) is the real rule; user_message follows 58/42 Hinglish ratio
Karnataka<->Lalbagh swap is realistic on SBC corridor; Hinglish register
Conditional Hinglish reasoning matches SemEval switch-point patterns
Pure Hinglish PNR-status query; canonical SemEval register
Hinglish register; child-berth policy is real IRCTC rule (5-12 yr child fare)
Post-chart cancellation must fail; charting-cutoff rule (~4h before departure) is real
AC Tatkal at non-10:00-IST time must be rejected; anchored to real window rule
Train 99999 is deliberately out-of-range (passenger 5xxxx, express 1xxxx); adversarial probe
Reserved template for any added scam-link adversarial; URL pattern + TLD prior anchored

split_summary.note: scenario_phishing_refund_link_template listed for source-mapping completeness but is not yet a file in scenarios/ directory; counts above exclude it. 14/18 of in-repo scenarios = 78% real-anchored, exceeds the 60/40 target.
